compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
`rebar bridge-fsck` reports "no issues found" while three real drift classes exist (a dangling confirmed binding, archived-local-with-live-Jira bindings, and an unbound Jira-native issue). Its checks are structurally blind to all of them.

## Root Cause (offline local-event scan; never reads bindings.json or Jira)
- `audit_bridge_mappings` (`bridge_fsck.py:59-183`) walks per-ticket event-log dirs under `.tickets-tracker/` only (`:102-127`); it has ZERO references to `bindings.json`, Jira, or acli.
- Its three checks: Orphan = SYNC-without-CREATE (`:137-139`); Duplicate = same `jira_key` on >=2 ticket dirs (`:141-143`,`:173-176`); Stale SYNC = latest SYNC >30d with no later BRIDGE_ALERT (`:145-171`). None can see: a confirmed binding whose Jira issue was deleted; a binding whose local ticket is archived while Jira stays live; or a Jira-native issue with no binding.
- The detector that WOULD catch these (`reconcile_check.py:229-259`, `orphaned_bindings` / `orphaned_jira`) is report-only and NOT on the `bridge-fsck` path; it also needs a live Jira fetch, so it cannot run offline.

## Detector landscape (from a full inventory)
- Two disjoint families: OFFLINE (`bridge-fsck`, local events only) and ONLINE (invariants `check_dual_identity_complete` `invariants.py:228`; differ `dd-5` dangling-jira-ref `differ.py:240,426`; `reconcile_check`). The healing-capable ones run ONLY inside a live reconcile pass; `reconcile_check` is the only binding-aware read-only detector but is report-only and needs Jira.
- No detector reads `bindings.json` in an offline path.
- Dead code: `bridge_fsck.enumerate_*` parallel enumerators (per `docs/maintenance-audit-runbook.md:121`).

## History (why the families are separate / unwired)
- Both `audit_bridge_mappings` and `reconcile_check` were introduced in the SAME commit `b534f47a7` ("Extract DSO ticket system + Jira reconciler into standalone rebar") — inherited VERBATIM from DSO, never engineered as a rebar decision.
- `e15907dd0` ("bridge-fsck audit in-process") ported `audit_bridge_mappings` as the "canonical" fast offline scan and deliberately left the one Jira-touching enumerator dead. `reconcile_check` never got an offline arm (docstring frames it as the live-fetch diagnostic; `docs/jira-sync-setup.md:144,389`).
- No commit/ADR/doc ever proposed unifying the families or giving `bridge-fsck` a bindings.json-aware mode.

## Reproduction Steps
1. With a dangling confirmed binding present (e.g. f8b5 -> deleted REB-530), run `rebar bridge-fsck`.
2. Observe "No issues found" despite the drift.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: `bridge-fsck` (or a companion audit) flags dangling bindings, archived-local-with-live-Jira bindings, and unbound Jira-native issues.
- Actual: it reports clean; the drift is invisible.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] An offline audit reads `bindings.json` and flags: (a) a confirmed binding whose Jira side is gone, (b) a binding whose local ticket is archived/deleted while Jira stays live, (c) [with a Jira snapshot] an unbound Jira-native issue.
- [ ] Either `bridge-fsck` gains a bindings.json-aware check, or `reconcile_check`'s binding-aware detectors are wired to a reachable audit surface (CLI/lib/MCP), with the offline/online split documented.
- [ ] The dead `bridge_fsck.enumerate_*` code is removed or wired.
- [ ] A RED test: a fixture with a dangling confirmed binding -> the audit flags it (currently returns clean).

## Notes
Discovered comparing Jira REB vs local. Files: `bridge_fsck.py`, `reconcile_check.py`, `__main__.py`, `invariants.py`, `differ.py`; docs `jira-sync-setup.md`, `maintenance-audit-runbook.md`.

## Guardrails (must-preserve — prior-art + archaeology; folds in the corrected plan)
- [ ] ONE pure classifier `classify(local, jira, binding, baseline) -> Decision`, TWO consumers: the live pass ACTS on it; the audit REPORTS it. Detection becomes the enforcement mechanism (no more report-only/healing fork).
- [ ] `bridge-fsck` reads `bindings.json` READ-ONLY and never becomes a write path that bypasses the rebar-id label-write audit (L9).
- [ ] Offline mode runs the matrix cells decidable without Jira (archived-local, dangling-local); online mode (with a Jira snapshot) runs the full matrix — folding in `reconcile_check`'s `orphaned_bindings`/`orphaned_jira` detectors (`reconcile_check.py:229-259`).
- [ ] Report mode is the PARITY ORACLE: it surfaces the true drift set that every heal is validated against; wire it to alert (open a ticket) on unhealed drift.
- [ ] Remove the dead `bridge_fsck.enumerate_*` parallel enumerators.

## Implementation contract (foundation-first — read before coding)
DEPENDS ON amiss-bun-wince (baf7): this bug delivers the SECOND CONSUMER of the one classifier — audit/report — healing the report-only/healing fork. One decision function, two callers: the live pass ACTS on Decisions; bridge-fsck/reconcile_check REPORT them.
1. Offline arm (no Jira): `rebar bridge-fsck` (src/rebar/_engine_support/bridge_fsck.py `audit_bridge_mappings`) additionally loads .tickets-tracker/.bridge_state/bindings.json + bindings-retired.json and runs the matrix cells decidable WITHOUT Jira: (a) bound + local archived/deleted (report as would-be TERMINAL_TRANSITION), (b) bound + local id absent from the store (ALERT), (c) binding entries vs retired overlap sanity. Import classify() from the reconciler package via the existing _engine_support loading pattern; bridge-fsck stays STRICTLY read-only (never writes labels — L9 audit boundary).
2. Online arm (Jira snapshot available): fold reconcile_check.py's orphaned_bindings/orphaned_jira detectors into the same classifier call — reconcile_check becomes `classify()` over (bindings x snapshot) with kind->finding rendering; delete its bespoke comparison for lifecycle findings (keep field-level compare).
3. Output: extend the bridge_fsck findings dict + src/rebar/schemas/bridge_fsck.schema.json with a `binding_drift` section {would_terminal[], dangling[], unbound_jira[], local_gone[]}; the MCP bridge_fsck read tool inherits it via the schema (docs/output-schemas.md pattern).
4. Remove the dead `bridge_fsck.enumerate_*` parallel enumerators (verified dead per docs/maintenance-audit-runbook.md).
5. Alerting: a non-empty binding_drift section from the scheduled canary (reconcile-bridge-canary.yml) opens/updates a bridge_alert — report mode is the PARITY ORACLE the epic's convergence claim is validated against.
RED test: fixture with a dangling confirmed binding + an archived-bound binding -> current audit returns clean -> new audit flags both. Golden-help updates for bridge-fsck output changes (tests/golden/cli_help/).
Live acceptance cells (2026-07-03 verified): the offline arm flags the 5 archived-bound REB-464/456/465/466/457 + f8b5->REB-530; the online arm additionally flags REB-532 unbound.
Files: bridge_fsck.py, reconcile_check.py, classify.py (consume only), schemas/bridge_fsck.schema.json, canary workflow, tests/scripts/bridge/test_bridge_fsck.py.
compiled_state.title: bridge-fsck is blind to binding-level drift (never reads bindings.json or Jira)

verify_commands.dd_text: dangling + archived-bound fixtures flagged (RED first: current audit clean)

verify_commands.command: rebar bridge-fsck  # binding_drift section non-empty until heals land
verify_commands.dd_text: offline arm flags the 6 known local-decidable instances on prod artifacts
